sections.paragraph:
f. verbal of Piel, from הָוָה-

(1) desire, cupidity, from the root No. 2, Pro 10:3, הַוַּת רְשָׁעִים יֶהְדֹּף “she casts away the desire of the wicked.” Parall. נֶפֶשׁ צַדִּיק. Comp. אַוָּה. (Arab. هَوًى desire, will), Pro 19:13 Job 6:2 30:13 (in these two latter places the קרי is הַיָּה). [See also No. 2, to which these three passages are also referred; in Thes. they are omitted under No. 1.]

(2) ruin, fall (Arab. هَوْىُ), from the root No. 3. Hence calamity, destruction. Psa 57:2, עַד־יַעֲבֹר הַוֹּות “until destruction be past;” Psal. 91:3 , דֶּבֶר הַוֹּות “the destroying pestilence;” Psa 94:20 Pro 19:13 Job 6:2 30:13 . Hence also, destruction which any one brings upon another, injury, mischief, wickedness. Psal. 5:10 , קִרְבָּם הַוֹּות “their inward part is very wickedness;” Psa 38:13, דִּבְּרוּ הַוֹּות “they speak of mischiefs;” Psa 52:4, Psa 52:9 55:12 Pro 11:6 17:4 , מֵזִין עַל־לְשֹׁון הַוֹּות “listening to a mischievous tongue;” Job 6:30.
